A truck carrying relief supplies overturned on Wednesday in the Gaza Strip, killing at least 20 Palestinians amid what has been described as 'Israeli-engineered' aid delivery chaos. According to local media, the Israeli army forces the trucks to take unsafe and unsecured routes that were previously bombed and rendered unfit for passage. The practice is part of what the Gaza-based Government Media Office called a policy of "engineering chaos" to maintain widespread starvation in the Gaza Strip. "This reveals the occupation's deliberate attempts to force civilians into danger and death as part of its engineering of chaos and starvation," the office said. Israel has been accused by leading human rights groups and experts of the crime of starving civilians since the beginning of its war on Gaza in October 2023.

Jordan’s Minister of Government Communication, Mohammad Momani, on August 6 strongly condemned repeated attacks on humanitarian aid convoys bound for Gaza, after illegal settlers assaulted a 30-truck convoy earlier that day in the occupied West Bank, damaging several vehicles and halting their progress. Calling out what he described as leniency toward illegal settler aggression, Momani urged immediate Israeli intervention, warning that such actions violate international law and endanger lives. He also highlighted mounting logistical hurdles faced by the Jordan Hashemite Charity Organization, including prolonged inspections, limited operating hours at border crossings, a complicated application process, and new customs fees of $300 – $400 per truck imposed since July 10.

It was to be expected that Israel, after the barbaric Hamas attack in which innocent Jews were murdered, women were raped and then killed — acts that even animals would not commit — would decide to take over the whole of Gaza. Both sides in this conflict have often killed innocent people, but Hamas displayed a level of brutality that will have a major impact on Israel’s decisions. The global opinion is also asking: why did Hamas do this? It was the greatest folly, one that deprived Palestine of a real chance to become a sovereign state. There are, of course, many actors who have taken away the hope for a free Palestine — such as Iran, Hezbollah, and other Islamists. I doubt that, after all this, Israel will agree to any kind of settlement. On the contrary, it is increasingly likely that Israel will take full control of Gaza.
